title: Large union (8 heterogeneous arms) — value-first infer fallback
description: Past the 4 positional union() overloads, the value-first builder routes through the recursive UnionOf<T> infer fallback. 8 arms (literals + primitives + a {a}/{a;b} subset+superset pair) verify the fallback BOTH generates a correct validator AND converges on the type-first union id — preserving the subset/superset arms (no subtype collapse) at depth 8.
notes: The `{a}`/`{a; b}` subset pair both stay reachable: a value matching the smaller `{a: string}` arm passes (e.g. `{a: "x"}` is valid), so the superset arm never swallows it. A failing value reports a single `expected: "union"` at the root, not per-arm errors.
pureType: createValidate<'a' | 'b' | number | boolean | null | {a: string} | {a: string; b: number} | {c: bigint}>()
schema:
Validate(
  RT.union([
    RT.literal('a'),
    RT.literal('b'),
    RT.number(),
    RT.boolean(),
    RT.literal(null),
    RT.object({a: RT.string()}),
    RT.object({a: RT.string(), b: RT.number()}),
    RT.object({c: RT.bigint()}),
  ])
)
generated: function YOw_umsOi6A(v){return (v === null || Number.isFinite(v) || v === 'a' || v === 'b' || v === false || v === true || (typeof v === 'object' && v !== null && ((typeof v === 'object' && v !== null && typeof v.a === 'string' && Number.isFinite(v.b)) || (typeof v === 'object' && v !== null && typeof v.a === 'string') || (typeof v === 'object' && v !== null && typeof v.c === 'bigint'))))}
